mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates indicate an accommodative monetary policy environment, reducing the cost of capital for corporations and encouraging investment, which translates to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for more than 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies an economic slowdown, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, signaling a healthy economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 5%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show sustained growth as specified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion indicates increasing productive capacity and strong business activity, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances during periods of confirmed economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth signifies a robust overall economy, indicating healthy corporate revenues and earnings, which supports higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and growing labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS increases by over 100,000 in a month.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly high inflation, as measured by CPI, can erode purchasing power and corporate profit margins, potentially leading to tighter monetary policy and negative sentiment for equities, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL shows a 1-month increase greater than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and slowing payroll growth indicates a significant weakening of the labor market, signaling an impending economic downturn that will negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months AND PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 50,000
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances where both UNRATE rises and PAYEMS growth slows below the specified thresholds.
